title: ononis hispida subsp. hispida
intro: Ononis hispida subsp. hispida, also known as the hairy restharrow, is a type of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The hairy restharrow is said to have various health benefits, such as relieving menstrual cramps, reducing fever, and soothing digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make infusions, tinctures, and ointments that can be applied topically to treat wounds and skin conditions.
